segments.text: Is an MBA still worth it in 2026?
India's top business schools are under
pressure. IM Ahmedabad and IM Kolkata
still top the rankings. But the real
story isn't rank, it's returns. Average
salaries at the top 25 B schools have
slipped from 23.12 lak rupees to 22.7
lak rupees [music] while average fees
have crossed 20 lak rupees. ROI is
shrinking quietly. Placements are
slower, internships are getting cut, and
global uncertainty combined with AI
[music] adoption is making companies
cautious about hiring even at top
campuses. So, what are the best B
schools doing? They're redesigning
classrooms. [music] Less road theory,
more real world cases, simulations,
industry projects, and AIdriven
decision-m because in 2026, companies
don't hire people with degrees. They
hire judgment. They hire problem
solvers. Firms like McKenzie still hire
heavily from top B schools. 77% of their
India hiring in the last 3 years comes
from these campuses. So in an AIdriven
world, is an MBA still relevant or just
expensive validation? Let me know in the
comments.
